The full Tabac superfamily spans well over 100 fonts, giving designers a coherent palette across radically different moods and settings.
foundry_blurb: Tabac Glam is part of the Tabac superfamily from Suitcase Type Foundry, a wide-ranging type system that includes Tabac, Tabac Slab, Tabac Sans, Tabac Mono and Tabac Big Glam, together spanning over 90 fonts across multiple styles and weights.

name: Tabac Micro
classification_notes: Part of the Tabac superfamily, Tabac Micro is specifically optimised for small-size legibility, placing it firmly in the optical serif subcategory designed for constrained reading environments.
summary: A micro-optimised serif from the Tabac superfamily, designed for small-size and demanding print environments.
description: Tabac Micro sits within Suitcase Type Foundry's extensive Tabac superfamily alongside Tabac, Tabac Sans, Tabac Slab and Tabac Mono. Its optical adjustments target very small print sizes where standard text faces begin to lose definition, making it suited to footnotes, captions and dense information design. The superfamily structure allows consistent typographic voice across a range of scales and applications.
foundry_blurb: Tabac Micro is part of the broader Tabac type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, which encompasses serif, sans, slab and mono variants across dozens of styles, offering a cohesive solution for complex typographic requirements.

name: Tabac Mono
classification_notes: Listed under the Tabac family alongside serif, sans and slab variants; the Mono designation confirms a monospaced design, consistent with the technical/coding mono subcategory.
summary: A monospaced member of the Tabac type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, designed for use alongside the broader Tabac family of serif, sans and slab companions.
description: Part of the extensive Tabac system, which spans sans, slab, micro and glam variants, the Mono brings a fixed-width option to an already well-developed type family. Its inclusion alongside optical-size-specific cuts like Tabac Micro suggests the system is intended for demanding editorial and information design contexts. Specific weights, italics and language coverage are not detailed on the page.
foundry_blurb: Tabac Mono is presented by Suitcase Type Foundry as part of the wider Tabac family, which includes Tabac, Tabac Glam, Tabac Micro, Tabac Sans and Tabac Slab, covering a broad range of typographic needs within a single cohesive system.

name: Tabac Sans
classification_notes: Part of the broader Tabac type system, Tabac Sans sits within a humanist sans tradition, designed to complement the serif and slab members of the family with matching proportions and spacing.
summary: A humanist sans component of the wider Tabac type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, designed to work in concert with the family's serif and slab variants.
description: Tabac Sans belongs to an extensive type system that includes Tabac, Tabac Slab, Tabac Glam, Tabac Micro, Tabac Mono and Tabac Big Sans, giving designers a coordinated toolkit across multiple genres. The sans variant is built for text and editorial use, sharing proportions with its siblings for consistent typographic colour across a publication or identity. The breadth of the system makes it particularly suited to complex publishing and branding projects requiring typographic consistency.
foundry_blurb: Tabac Sans is part of Suitcase Type Foundry's Tabac type system, a large family spanning multiple subfamilies including Tabac, Tabac Slab, Tabac Glam, Tabac Micro, Tabac Mono and Tabac Big Sans, offering a comprehensive range of styles for editorial and identity work.

name: Tabac Slab
classification_notes: Part of the extended Tabac superfamily, the Slab variant applies bracketed or geometric slab serifs to what is otherwise a humanist-influenced text family, placing it squarely in the slab serif category.
summary: A slab serif member of Suitcase Type Foundry's wide-ranging Tabac superfamily, designed for text-weight editorial use.
description: Tabac Slab sits within a broad type system that spans sans, slab, glam, micro and mono variants, suggesting a family built for sustained publishing environments where multiple voices are needed. The slab cut offers serif warmth and structure suited to body text and mid-scale display. Related families like Tabac Micro and Tabac Mono hint at serious attention to optical sizing and functional range across the system.
foundry_blurb: Tabac Slab is part of the extensive Tabac type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, a superfamily that includes Tabac, Tabac Sans, Tabac Glam, Tabac Big Slab, Tabac Micro and Tabac Mono, offering broad stylistic range for complex editorial and publishing projects.

name: Teimer

summary: A serif typeface from Czech foundry Suitcase Type, with limited detail available from the product page.

name: Yolo Body
classification_notes: The Yolo family is structured across optical size ranges (Info, Text, Body, Display), suggesting a contemporary serif designed for multi-scale use; Body sits in the mid-range for general reading text.
summary: The body-text optical cut of the Yolo serif family, sitting between the smaller Text and the larger Display variants.
description: Yolo Body is one of four optically tuned cuts in the Yolo family from Suitcase Type Foundry, spanning Info, Text, Body and Display. Each subfamily comprises ten fonts, suggesting a range of weights with matching italics across each optical size. The Body cut is calibrated for mid-scale reading contexts where the Text size would be too fine and the Display too loose.
foundry_blurb: Yolo Body is part of the wider Yolo family from Suitcase Type Foundry, which includes four related subfamilies — Yolo Info, Yolo Text, Yolo Body and Yolo Display — each comprising ten fonts and optimised for different optical sizes and reading conditions.

name: Yolo Display
classification_notes: Classified as a display typeface given its name explicitly includes 'Display', suggesting it is optimised for large sizes and headline use, part of a broader Yolo family that includes body and text variants.
summary: The display-optimised member of Suitcase Type Foundry's Yolo family, intended for headline and large-scale use alongside companion body and text cuts.
description: Part of a four-subfamily system that spans Display, Text, Body and Info, each cut tuned for its intended size range. The Display variant offers 10 fonts, suggesting a full weight range with italics for expressive headline work. The tiered family structure indicates careful attention to optical performance across different typesetting contexts.
foundry_blurb: Yolo Display is part of the wider Yolo type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, which includes Yolo Info, Yolo Body and Yolo Text, together comprising a complete typographic family across a range of optical sizes and uses.

name: Yolo Info
classification_notes: The Info subfamily of the Yolo system suggests a functional, information-oriented cut typical of humanist sans designs intended for UI, wayfinding or data-dense contexts.
summary: The Info cut of a four-subfamily type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, optimised for data-dense and information-heavy settings.
description: Yolo Info is one of four optical subfamily variants within the Yolo system, which also includes Body, Text and Display cuts, each tuned to a different reading environment and size range. The Info subfamily is positioned for functional, information-oriented use cases such as UI, wayfinding or tabular content. With ten fonts in the subfamily, the family offers sufficient weight range for complex typographic hierarchies.
foundry_blurb: Yolo Info is part of the broader Yolo type system from Suitcase Type Foundry, which comprises four related subfamilies: Yolo Info, Yolo Body, Yolo Text and Yolo Display, each available in ten fonts.

name: Yolo Text
classification_notes: The Yolo family is organised into optical size variants (Info, Body, Text, Display), suggesting a contemporary text serif designed for specific size ranges. The Text subfamily implies optimised proportions and spacing for mid-range body copy use.
summary: A text-optimised serif from Suitcase Type Foundry, part of a four-subfamily system spanning optical sizes from Info to Display.
description: Yolo Text sits within a larger optical size family comprising Info, Body, Text and Display cuts, each tuned for its intended size range. The family counts 10 fonts per subfamily, suggesting a thorough weight range suited to continuous reading and editorial layout. The system is structured for publishing and multi-context typesetting where optical consistency across sizes matters.
